perturbation.original_text: You and your representatives will not use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose other than the exclusive purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction. You and your representatives will keep the Evaluation Materials completely confidential and will not disclose the Evaluation Materials, in any manner, in whole or in part; provided, however, that (i) any of such information may only be disclosed to those of your directors, officers, employees, agents, representatives (including attorneys, accountants and financial advisors), lenders and other sources of financing (collectively, "your representatives") who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction between you and the Company (it being understood that your representatives shall be informed by you of the confidential nature of such information and shall be directed by you and shall agree to treat such information confidentially in accordance with this Agreement) and (ii) any other disclosure of such information may only be made if the Company consents in writing prior to any such disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: You and your representatives will not use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose other than the exclusive purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction. You and your representatives will keep the Evaluation Materials completely confidential and will not disclose the Evaluation Materials, in any manner, in whole or in part; provided, however, that (i) any of such information may only be disclosed to those of your directors, officers, employees, agents, representatives (including attorneys, accountants and financial advisors), lenders and other sources of financing (collectively, "your representatives") who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction between you and the Company (it being understood that your representatives shall be informed by you of the confidential nature of such information and shall be directed by you and shall agree to treat such information confidentially in accordance with this Agreement).
perturbation.explanation: By removing clause (ii), which allowed for disclosure with the Company's prior written consent, the contract now creates an in-text contradiction with the statement in the LEGAL REMEDY section which implies that 'the Company' can bring legal action against the other party for disclosure. This creates an ambiguity as to whether disclosure is ever permissible, even with consent, leading to uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You understand and agree that the Company would be irreparably injured and that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by you or your representatives and that the Company will be entitled to seek specific performance and injunctive relief as remedies for any such breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement by you or your representatives but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for disclosure of Evaluation Materials with the Company's written consent (clause ii). The changed text removes this clause, creating a direct conflict with the 'LEGAL REMEDY' section. The 'LEGAL REMEDY' section implies that the Company can bring legal action against the other party for any breach of the agreement, seemingly including unauthorized disclosure. However, without clause (ii), it's now unclear if disclosure is ever permissible, even with written consent. This creates an ambiguity about the enforceability of the agreement and introduces an in-text contradiction, making the obligations concerning disclosure unclear.

perturbation.original_text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, in the event that a Possible Transaction is not consummated neither you nor your representatives shall use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose. You will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by your representatives. In the event that you or any of your representatives receive a request or are required (by deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process) to <PAGE> 2 disclose all or any part of the Evaluation Materials, you or your representatives, as the case may be, agree to (i) immediately notify the Company of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request, (ii) consult with the Company on the advisability of taking legally available steps to resist or narrow such request and (iii) assist the Company (at the Company's expense) in seeking a protective order or other appropriate remedy.
perturbation.changed_text: Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, in the event that a Possible Transaction is not consummated neither you nor your representatives shall use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose. You will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by your representatives. In the event that you or any of your representatives receive a request or are required (by deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process) to <PAGE> 2 disclose all or any part of the Evaluation Materials, you or your representatives, as the case may be, agree to (i) immediately notify the Company of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request, (ii) consult with the Company on the advisability of taking legally available steps to resist or narrow such request.
perturbation.explanation: By removing clause (iii), which states the other party should 'assist the Company (at the Company's expense) in seeking a protective order or other appropriate remedy,' an in-text contradiction is created when cross referenced with the LEGAL REMEDY section. While the former section stated that the company is allowed to take action when the agreement is breached, by removing the other party to assist in seeking a protective order, the other party can claim that they were never required to assist, thus there was no breach in agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You understand and agree that the Company would be irreparably injured and that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by you or your representatives and that the Company will be entitled to seek specific performance and injunctive relief as remedies for any such breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement by you or your representatives but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity.
perturbation.justification: The removal of clause (iii) from the original text, which required the other party to assist the Company in seeking a protective order, directly contradicts the 'LEGAL REMEDY' section. The LEGAL REMEDY section states that the Company can seek remedies for any breach of the agreement. By removing the obligation to assist in obtaining a protective order, the changed text makes it difficult for the Company to prove a breach related to this specific requirement, thus impacting the enforceability of the agreement and contradicting the Company's right to seek remedies for such a breach as outlined in the LEGAL REMEDY section.

perturbation.original_text: RETURN OF DOCUMENTS Upon the Company's request, you shall promptly deliver to the Company or destroy all written Evaluation Materials and any other written materials without retaining, in whole or in part, any copies, extracts or other reproductions (whatever the form or storage medium) of such materials, and shall certify the destruction of such materials in writing to the Company. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Materials, you and your representatives will continue to be bound by your obligations pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: RETURN OF DOCUMENTS Upon the Company's request, you shall promptly deliver to the Company or destroy all written Evaluation Materials and any other written materials without retaining, in whole or in part, any copies, extracts or other reproductions (whatever the form or storage medium) of such materials. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Materials, you and your representatives will continue to be bound by your obligations pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'and shall certify the destruction of such materials in writing to the Company', it creates uncertainty and an in-text contradiction. While the main clause states that one must deliver or destroy all documents, by removing the phrase 'certify the destruction of such materials', the other party could claim they destroyed or delivered the materials, while the company cannot verify. Furthermore, it opens the door to an interpretation that the party is only required to return or destroy the 'Evaluation Materials' but not 'any other written materials,' effectively negating the initial broader obligation. This introduces ambiguity because the company cannot ensure or enforce the destruction of the documents
perturbation.contradicted_text: You understand and agree that the Company would be irreparably injured and that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by you or your representatives and that the Company will be entitled to seek specific performance and injunctive relief as remedies for any such breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement by you or your representatives but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement to 'certify the destruction of such materials in writing to the Company'. This creates an in-text contradiction because without the certification, the Company's ability to enforce the agreement and seek remedies for a breach, as stated in the 'Contradicted Text' is weakened. The Company can no longer definitively prove non-compliance, thus hindering its ability to seek specific performance and injunctive relief. This aligns with the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition, as essential language has been removed, creating a contradiction with obligations stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: STANDSTILL You agree that until one year from the date of this Agreement, you will not without the prior approval of the Board of Directors of the Company (i) acquire or make any proposal to acquire any securities or property of the Company, (ii) propose to enter into any merger or business combination involving the Company or purchase a material portion of the assets of the Company, (iii) make or participate in any solicitation of proxies to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of any securities of the Company, (iv) form, join or participate in a "group" (within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934) with respect to any voting securities of the Company, (v) otherwise act or seek to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or 2 <PAGE> 3 policies of the Company, (vi) disclose any intention, plan or arrangement inconsistent with the foregoing or (vii) take any action which might require the Company to make a public announcement regarding the possibility of a business combination or merger. Except as provided above, you also agree during such period not to request the Company (or its directors, officers, employees, agents or representatives) to amend or waive any provision of this paragraph.
perturbation.changed_text: STANDSTILL You agree that until one year from the date of this Agreement, you will not without the prior approval of the Board of Directors of the Company (i) acquire or make any proposal to acquire any securities or property of the Company, (ii) propose to enter into any merger or business combination involving the Company or purchase a material portion of the assets of the Company, (iii) make or participate in any solicitation of proxies to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of any securities of the Company, (iv) form, join or participate in a "group" (within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934) with respect to any voting securities of the Company, (v) otherwise act or seek to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or 2 <PAGE> 3 policies of the Company, (vi) disclose any intention, plan or arrangement inconsistent with the foregoing.
perturbation.explanation: By removing clause (vii), 'take any action which might require the Company to make a public announcement regarding the possibility of a business combination or merger' from the standstill agreement, it creates a contradiction because it is already stated that 'you will not, and you will direct your representatives not to, disclose to any person either the fact that you have entered into this Agreement'. Since clause (vii) is omitted it now becomes conflicting because even if a public announcement happens, it cannot be claimed that this is a breach of agreement and there may not be a remedy or legal right for 'the company'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Accordingly, unless required by applicable law, you agree that prior to the closing of a Possible Transaction, without the prior written consent of the Company, you will not, and you will direct your representatives not to, disclose to any person either the fact that you have entered into this Agreement, that Evaluation Materials have been made available or that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a Possible Transaction between you and the Company or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such Possible Transaction, including the status thereof.
perturbation.justification: The omission of clause (vii) in the changed text, which originally prevented actions requiring public announcements regarding business combinations or mergers, directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text'. The contradicted text explicitly prohibits disclosing the existence of the agreement or discussions about a possible transaction. By removing (vii), actions leading to mandatory public announcements, which would inherently disclose those very facts, are no longer explicitly prohibited by the standstill agreement, thereby creating a contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' where essential language is removed creating a contradiction with obligations or constraints stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: You also acknowledge and agree that no contract or agreement providing for a Possible Transaction shall be deemed to exist between you and the Company unless and until a Sale Agreement has been executed and delivered by you and each of the other parties thereto. You also agree that unless and until a Sale Agreement between the Company and you with respect to a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered by you and each of the other parties thereto, there shall not be any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to any such transaction by virtue of this agreement or any other written or oral expression with respect to such transaction except, in the case of this Agreement, for the matters specifically agreed to herein. For purposes of this Agreement, the term "Sale Agreement" does not include an executed letter of intent or any other preliminary written agreement, nor does it include any oral acceptance of an offer or bid by you.
perturbation.changed_text: You also acknowledge and agree that no contract or agreement providing for a Possible Transaction shall be deemed to exist between you and the Company unless and until a Sale Agreement has been executed and delivered by you and each of the other parties thereto. You also agree that unless and until a Sale Agreement between the Company and you with respect to a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered by you and each of the other parties thereto, there shall not be any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to any such transaction by virtue of this agreement or any other written or oral expression with respect to such transaction except, in the case of this Agreement, for the matters specifically agreed to herein.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the sentence 'For purposes of this Agreement, the term "Sale Agreement" does not include an executed letter of intent or any other preliminary written agreement, nor does it include any oral acceptance of an offer or bid by you', this now creates an in-text contradiction by opening the door to interpret 'Sale Agreement' to include a 'letter of intent' or 'oral acceptance of an offer'. This contradicts the intention of requiring a fully executed agreement because now there is uncertainty as to what constitutes an actual 'Sale Agreement', and whether that is only 'Sale Agreement' or now the definition has been broadened.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Only those representations or warranties that are made in a definitive agreement relating to the Company ("Sale Agreement") when, as, and if it is executed, and subject to such limitations and restrictions as may be specified in such Sale Agreement, will have any legal effect.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly excludes preliminary agreements like letters of intent from the definition of 'Sale Agreement'. By removing this exclusion in the changed text, the definition of 'Sale Agreement' becomes ambiguous. This ambiguity directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text', which states that only representations or warranties made in a 'Sale Agreement' (as previously defined) will have legal effect. The removal allows preliminary agreements to potentially be considered a 'Sale Agreement', creating a contradiction and uncertainty about which agreements have legal effect, thus falling under the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.
